name: New Southwestern Corn Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup grated Cheddar cheese, or Colby
2 egg whites, lightly beaten with eggs
1/3 cup cornmeal mix, or self-rising cornmeal
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Butter a 1 1/2-quart soufflé dish.
3. Cook the corn in the husk for 2 minutes on high in a microwave oven, then cool for a minute or two before removing the husk.
4. Alternatively, place shucked corn in boiling water until cooked through (about 5 minutes).
5. Using a sharp knife, cut the corn from the cob and scrape the cob to extract the "milk."
6. Combine the corn, milk, cottage cheese, Monterey Jack cheese, Cheddar cheese, eggs, onion, chile peppers, cornmeal mix, and cilantro in a bowl.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Chill for 4 hours at this stage.
9. Pour mixture into the prepared soufflé dish.
10. Place a damp kitchen towel in a baking pan and set the soufflé dish on the towel.
11. Add hot water to the pan until it reaches two-thirds up the sides of the dish.
12. Bake for 1 hour or until lightly browned and a cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
13. Serve hot.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
